message:
perf(render): replace format!/write! with hand-rolled byte-buffer ANSI emission

Eliminate allocation and formatting overhead on the render hot path
by replacing write!/format! macro calls with direct byte-buffer
construction for ANSI escape sequences:

- Add write_u32_dec(): format u32 to stack buffer without allocation
  (complements existing write_u8_dec for values >255)
- Add write_sgr_rgb_seq(): batch entire SGR true-color escape
  sequence (\x1b[38;2;r;g;bm or \x1b[48;2;r;g;bm) into a single
  write_all call using a stack buffer, replacing two write!() calls
  in sgr_fg_rgb and sgr_bg_rgb
- Add write_csi_u32_suffix(): generic helper for CSI sequences of
  the form \x1b[<number><letter>, used by cursor movement commands
- Rewrite cup() (cursor position), cha(), cuu(), cud(), cuf(), cub()
  to build sequences in stack buffers instead of using write!
- Add tests verifying new paths match format!-based reference output
  for boundary values (0, 1, u16::MAX) and various RGB combinations

Also bump RowPlan SmallVec inline capacity from 4 to 8 RowSpan
entries (24→48 bytes) to reduce heap allocations for sparse rows
with several isolated dirty runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
